name: Crayfish Etouffee
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy pot, melt butter and sauté onions, celery, green pepper, and garlic until soft.
2. Stir in salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, sugar, and tomato paste. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes.
3. Dissolve cornstarch in water; add white wine to the mixture. Cook for 20 minutes or until the sauce thickens.
4. Add crayfish, chopped green onion, and Kitchen bouquet. Refrigerate overnight.
5. Reheat and serve over rice. Do not overcook.
6. For boiling crayfish: Discard any dead crayfish in the sack. Wash crayfish in clean, cool water just before cooking. They do not need to be purged with salt during washing.
7. Place live crayfish in boiling water and boil for 10 minutes. (Season the water with one pound of salt per five gallons of water. Add other seasonings to taste, including cayenne, garlic, onions, lemons, lemon juice, and crab boil.)
8. Crayfish are easiest to peel when still warm.
